summary: Comprehensive testing of Telehealth video call notification system. All critical features verified working: WebSocket connection, video call initiation, provider notifications, and call cancellation flow.

minor.issue: Returns HTML instead of JSON (likely ingress routing issue)
Returns HTML instead of JSON (likely ingress routing issue)

critical_code_review_comments: WebSocket setupWebSocket() function is now correctly called in useEffect at lines 83-96 - FIX VERIFIED
CSP in index.html now includes 'https:' in connect-src directive - FIX VERIFIED
Provider receives incoming_video_call notifications via WebSocket - WORKING
Call cancellation flow properly dismisses modal and stops ringing - WORKING
seed_data_creation: Test appointments created during testing were cleaned up
context_for_next_testing_agent: All video call notification features are working. WebSocket connection established successfully. Provider receives incoming_video_call notifications. Call cancellation properly handled. Polling working every 2 seconds.

provider_login_and_websocket.evidence: Console logs show 'Provider WebSocket connected successfully - ALWAYS ONLINE MODE'

doctor_login_and_video_call.evidence: Doctor can click Emergency Call button, backend returns success with Jitsi URL

provider_receiving_call_notification.evidence: Console logs show 'Received WhatsApp-like video call invitation' and modal appears with Answer/Decline buttons

call_notification_popup.evidence: Screenshot shows Video Call Invitation modal with caller info, patient info, and Answer/Decline buttons

call_cancellation_flow.evidence: Console logs show 'Doctor cancelled the call' and 'Call cancellation handled - modal dismissed and ringing stopped'

polling_working.evidence: Backend logs show appointments fetched every 2 seconds

websocket_setup_not_called.description: useEffect now calls setupWebSocket() when user is available

csp_blocking_connections.description: CSP connect-src now includes 'https:' to allow external API connections
